title: Fresh Crunchy Portuguese Garlic Sushi
description: A fusion of traditional Portuguese flavors with the crunch of sushi. This dish combines the pungency of garlic with the zing of yuzu-cure, making for a refreshing and adventurous snack.

ingredients.name: Cauliflower
ingredients.notes: medium-sized, preferably with a tight, compact head

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: minced, using a microplane for best flavor

ingredients.name: Yuzu juice
ingredients.notes: freshly squeezed, or store-bought yuzu juice as a substitute

ingredients.name: Rice vinegar
ingredients.notes: white rice vinegar, for pickling the cauliflower

ingredients.name: Coconut oil
ingredients.notes: melted for brushing the cauliflower and forming the sushi rolls

ingredients.name: Chickpea flour
ingredients.notes: used for dusting the sushi rolls to help them stick together

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: used as a seasoning for the cauliflower and sushi rolls

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: used as a seasoning for the cauliflower and sushi rolls

instructions: Pulse the cauliflower in a food processor until it resembles rice.
Transfer the cauliflower rice to a bowl and mix in the minced garlic, yuzu juice, and rice vinegar.
Season the mixture with salt and black pepper to taste.
Using a bamboo sushi mat, shape the cauliflower mixture into a log and slice it into individual sushi rolls.
Dust each roll with chickpea flour to help them stick together.
Melt the coconut oil in a non-stick pan over medium heat.
Brush the cauliflower rolls with the melted coconut oil and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, until crispy.
Transfer the sushi rolls to a plate and garnish with chopped parsley.
Store leftover sushi rolls in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
When ready to serve, let the sushi rolls sit at room temperature for 30 minutes to allow them to crisp up again.
